page: Amazon Web Services r7i.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core supporting hyper-threading) running at 3.2 GHz. The CPU includes 105 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1909.48 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1691.0. However, due to its dual-vCPU configuration, multi-core performance is limited, as shown by a weak Geekbench multi-core score of 2066.0 and a poor stress-ng multi-core score of 1972.48. Memory bandwidth tests also show lower throughput on large blocks, with read and write speeds around 11 GB/sec. Text processing and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels, while database operations and software compilation show lower relative performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7i.large provides access to modern DDR5 memory and Intel AMX instructions at a lower entry-level resource scale. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel compute power. Recommended workloads include small-scale caching layers, light database operations, text processing, and memory-intensive utility tasks that benefit from DDR5 speeds but are constrained by a two-vCPU limit.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, running on a dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance provides an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio and supports advanced CPU instructions like Intel AMX and AVX-512. It contains no local storage or GPU accelerators, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average single-core execution in Geekbench and stress-ng, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics reflect the constraints of its dual-vCPU profile. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads with low parallel processing demands, such as small database instances, caching layers, and text processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8488C processor, designed for memory-bound workloads with low compute requirements.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A dual-vCPU memory-optimized instance featuring DDR5 memory and Intel AMX support for memory-bound workloads with low parallel compute needs.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 2 vCPUs and 105 MB L3 cache
16.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Support for Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Best suited for small-scale databases, caching, and text processing
